title: Delete Columns to Make Sorted III

testcases.input: strs = ["babca","bbazb"] Output: 3 Explanation: After deleting columns 0, 1, and 4, the final array is strs = ["bc", "az"]. Both these rows are individually in lexicographic order (ie. strs[0][0] <= strs[0][1] and strs[1][0] <= strs[1][1]). Note that strs[0] > strs[1] - the array strs is not necessarily in lexicographic order.
testcases.expectedOutput: 3 Explanation: After deleting columns 0, 1, and 4, the final array is strs = ["bc", "az"]. Both these rows are individually in lexicographic order (ie. strs[0][0] <= strs[0][1] and strs[1][0] <= strs[1][1]). Note that strs[0] > strs[1] - the array strs is not necessarily in lexicographic order.

testcases.input: strs = ["edcba"] Output: 4 Explanation: If we delete less than 4 columns, the only row will not be lexicographically sorted.
testcases.expectedOutput: 4 Explanation: If we delete less than 4 columns, the only row will not be lexicographically sorted.

testcases.input: strs = ["ghi","def","abc"] Output: 0 Explanation: All rows are already lexicographically sorted.
testcases.expectedOutput: 0 Explanation: All rows are already lexicographically sorted.
